candidates.source_name: EU AI Act, Article 14 — Human Oversight
candidates.primary_principle: High-risk AI systems must be designed so natural persons can effectively monitor operation, intervene, and override outputs in real time.
candidates.citation_form: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 of the European Parliament — AI Act, Article 14.
candidates.extracted_excerpt: 1. High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way, including with appropriate human-machine interface tools, that they can be effectively overseen by natural persons during the period in which they are in use.

candidates.author_or_publisher: U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology
candidates.primary_principle: Govern, Map, Measure, Manage: four functions that operationalize human-safe AI across the full system lifecycle.
candidates.citation_form: NIST (2023). AI Risk Management Framework 1.0. NIST AI 100-1.

candidates.primary_principle: AI systems should support inclusive growth, human-centered values, transparency, robustness, accountability, and democratic governance.
candidates.citation_form: OECD (2019). OECD Principles on Artificial Intelligence. oecd.ai.
candidates.extracted_excerpt: OECD.AI helps countries and shape trustworthy AI with the OECD AI Principles. It gives access to 900+ national AI policies and initiatives, live data about AI and a blog about AI policy.

candidates.primary_principle: Manage AI through an auditable management system covering policies, risk controls, lifecycle processes, and continual improvement.
candidates.citation_form: ISO/IEC 42001:2023. Information technology - Artificial intelligence - Management system.
candidates.extracted_excerpt: Information technology — Artificial intelligence — Management system

candidates.source_name: Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights
candidates.author_or_publisher: White House Office of Science and Technology Policy
candidates.primary_principle: People should have safe and effective systems, algorithmic discrimination protections, data privacy, notice, explanation, and human alternatives.
candidates.citation_form: White House OSTP (2022). Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights.
candidates.extracted_excerpt: Among the great challenges posed to democracy today is the use of technology, data, and automated systems in ways that threaten the rights of the American public. Too often, these tools are used to limit our opportunities and prevent our access to critical resources or services. These problems are well documented. In America and around…

candidates.primary_principle: Use a structured risk taxonomy to avoid missing known AI harm categories during governance review.
candidates.citation_form: MIT FutureTech. AI Risk Repository. airisk.mit.edu.
candidates.extracted_excerpt: The repository is part of the MIT AI Risk Initiative, which aims to increase awareness and adoption of best practice AI risk management across the AI ecosystem.
